author: mryzhov
title: RISCV64 Docker build fails - libpython3-dev:riscv64 has unresolvable dependencies on libtinfo6:riscv64
signature: apt-get install libpython3-dev:riscv64 fails - libpython3.10-stdlib:riscv64 depends on libtinfo6:riscv64 but not installable|Configuration

failed_jobs.name: Docker
failed_jobs.symptom: docker buildx build fails at Dockerfile step 5/8 - apt-get install libpython3-dev:riscv64 exits with code 100

key_errors: #12 8.550  libpython3.10-stdlib:riscv64 : Depends: libncursesw6:riscv64 (>= 6.1) but it is not going to be installed
#12 8.550  libpython3.10-stdlib:riscv64 : Depends: libtinfo6:riscv64 (>= 6) but it is not going to be installed
#12 8.550  libreadline8:riscv64 : Depends: libtinfo6:riscv64 (>= 6) but it is not going to be installed
#12 8.559 E: Unable to correct problems, you have held broken packages.

ERROR: failed to solve: process "/bin/sh -c dpkg --add-architecture riscv64 && apt-get update ... && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends libpython3-dev:riscv64" did not complete successfully: exit code: 100
RuntimeError: Command 'docker buildx build ... --file .github/dockerfiles/ov_build/ubuntu_22_04_riscv/Dockerfile ...' returned non-zero exit status 1
root_cause: The Dockerfile at .github/dockerfiles/ov_build/ubuntu_22_04_riscv/Dockerfile fails at step 5/8 where it installs libpython3-dev:riscv64 via cross-compilation. The libpython3.10-stdlib:riscv64 package depends on libncursesw6:riscv64 and libtinfo6:riscv64, but these cannot be installed because libtinfo6:amd64 was already installed in step 3/8 with different package constraints. The apt-get update with -o Dir::Etc::sourcelist= flag does not fully isolate the riscv64 sources (sources.list.d directory is still scanned, causing duplicate warnings), and the package dependency graph for libpython3-dev:riscv64 conflicts with the already-installed amd64 packages. This is a Dockerfile configuration issue with the apt cross-architecture setup.
reproduction_steps: docker buildx build --file .github/dockerfiles/ov_build/ubuntu_22_04_riscv/Dockerfile . (fails at RUN dpkg --add-architecture riscv64 && apt-get update ... && apt-get install libpython3-dev:riscv64 step)
recommended_actions: Inspect .github/dockerfiles/ov_build/ubuntu_22_04_riscv/Dockerfile lines 71-74 to understand the riscv64 apt cross-compilation setup
Add libncursesw6:riscv64 and libtinfo6:riscv64 explicitly to the apt-get install command in Dockerfile step 5/8 to satisfy the libpython3-dev:riscv64 dependencies
Consider using --no-install-recommends with explicit package list or pinning package versions to avoid cross-arch conflicts
Alternatively, restructure the Dockerfile to install riscv64 packages before amd64 packages to avoid held-package conflicts
prevention_strategies: When setting up cross-architecture apt in Dockerfiles, ensure that shared library packages (like libtinfo6, libncursesw6) are explicitly listed with both architectures, or use architecture-specific apt source pinning to avoid version conflicts between amd64 and riscv64 variants.
historical_context: No prior occurrences of this specific RISCV Docker build failure pattern in the CI Doctor MQ database.
